description: Added and registered `lowrank_two_stage_residual` in `sparsify/config.py` and `sparsify/sparse_coder.py`. The new class keeps the stable low-rank trunk, splits total K across two sparse residual passes, and reuses a shared decoder for both stages. Exact integration smoke test passed for `ARCHITECTURE=lowrank_two_stage_residual`, `K=32`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12` with forward+backward on CUDA. Recommended next run is a controlled full experiment at the established low-K recipe settings.
self_review: This is a clean single-variable architecture addition with whitelist and factory wiring completed. Main residual risk is scientific rather than integration: the 50/50 stage split may not be optimal, but it is the smallest defensible first probe and passed instantiate/forward/backward sanity.

description: Use a parameter-only follow-up on the already integrated `lowrank_two_stage_residual` family. Keep optimizer, LR, EF, and AUXK fixed; change only K from 32 to 24 to test the smaller-K tradeoff lane.
self_review: This respects the one-variable rule and directly targets the stated frontier objective. It avoids additional code churn after the prior family integration and asks the most decision-relevant question next: whether the family still holds up at a meaningfully smaller K.

description: Variable isolation violated: Multiple primary variables: architecture, k, ef, lr

Run a parameter-only follow-up on `lowrank_two_stage_residual` at `K=16`, with the rest of the recipe pinned to the current low-K baseline (`EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=1.6e-3`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0`). This directly repairs the prior policy-rejected round by isolating `K` as the sole changed variable.
self_review: This is the cleanest next probe: it targets the current frontier objective, changes one primary variable, and avoids recipe drift by explicitly pinning the baseline fields.

description: Continue the same-family low-K sweep with a parameter-only run at `K=8`, while pinning the existing low-K recipe (`ARCHITECTURE=lowrank_two_stage_residual`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=1.6e-3`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0`). The `K=16` result was good enough to justify tracing the tail lower before switching families or introducing routing changes.
self_review: This follows the current frontier logic cleanly: one variable changes, the recipe stays controlled, and the next most informative question is whether the same family still holds up at substantially smaller support.

description: Continue the controlled same-family sweep with a parameter-only run at `K=4`, while pinning the established recipe (`ARCHITECTURE=lowrank_two_stage_residual`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=1.6e-3`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0`). The `K=8` result remained keep-worthy, so the next most informative move is to finish tracing the low-K tail on the same family.
self_review: This preserves variable isolation, extends the existing Pareto mapping cleanly, and avoids switching families before the current low-K lane is fully characterized.

description: Continue the controlled same-family sweep with a parameter-only run at `K=2`, while pinning the established recipe (`ARCHITECTURE=lowrank_two_stage_residual`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=1.6e-3`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0`). The `K=4` result was still keep-worthy, so the next most informative move is to probe the next lower support level before changing architecture.

description: Continue the controlled same-family sweep with a parameter-only run at `K=1`, while pinning the established recipe (`ARCHITECTURE=lowrank_two_stage_residual`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=1.6e-3`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0`).
